name: Benny Golson
description: American bebop/hard bop jazz tenor saxophonist, composer, and arranger (1929–2024)
abstract: Benny Golson (January 25, 1929 – September 21, 2024) was an American bebop and hard bop jazz tenor saxophonist, composer, and arranger. He came to prominence with the big bands of Lionel Hampton and Dizzy Gillespie, more as a writer than a performer, before launching his solo career.

interviews.title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

concerts.label: 20th Century Innovations: Recent Developments in Jazz

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: So I said, 'Cedar I’ve been with my wife almost fifty years, maybe because of you.' He said, 'Brother Mabern, I didn’t know that.' So that’s how, and then I stayed with the band until it disbanded. I was with the band, we did two records together with the band
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: Through all of my connections, I put together one of the greatest jazz orchestras that ever was. ... Benny Golson was. Joe Newman was. Clark Terry was.
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Roy McCurdy
about_quotes.evidence: I got a telegram from Benny Golson. And he said he wanted me to come to New York and join the Jazztet... you got the job... Art and Benny and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy McCurdy interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Then when we saw Tadd Dameron in Atlantic City, and he went in Tadd’s band – Gigi, Benny Golson, and Clifford... I sit next to Brownie for a year. It was like an experience beyond description. Art Farmer. And Benny.
about_quotes.doc_title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I also have worked with the band called Roots... And then there’s Benny Golson... being with Benny Golson and playing those Benny Golson arrangements
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: The sextet was an outgrowth of what Tom McIntosh was interested in doing writing wise. So, originally it was Tom McIntosh, Benny Golson and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
